behavior_summary: U8P_KINGBDNS::look services the object's 'U8P_KINGBDNS::look' (examine) event: it prepares the object for an inspection, makes the item emit a short text 'bed ' (via an Item::bark call), records the bark return value, uses that result to indicate the event was handled, then suspends/returns.

Before barking, the routine performs a 'set info' with a constant (observed as 0x0208) on the object and applies a process-exclude-like operation; these likely configure UI/processing state so the U8P_KINGBDNS::look interaction is handled cleanly and other processors won't immediately reprocess the object.

The routine passes the object pointer and a process id/context (pid) along with the string pointer for 'bed ' to an external Item::bark-like routine, captures its return value, and uses an 'implies' operation to produce the final return value that signals the event was handled.
notable_control_flow: Entry triggered by Event 0 ('U8P_KINGBDNS::look') for the object; routine runs synchronously in the object's process context.

Sequence: set-info(0x0208) on the object -> process-exclude on the object -> push parameters (object pointer, string 'bed ', pid) -> call Item::bark -> capture return value -> apply 'implies' to indicate the event was handled -> suspend/return.
The handler does not set or read any global flags (no global reads/writes were observed).
open_questions: Exact semantic effect of the set-info constant 0x0208: what UI/interaction state or gameplay tag does it represent?
Precise semantics of 'process exclude' in this context: does it stop other scripts, prevent immediate reprocessing, or control visibility?
Exact parameter ordering and types for the Item::bark call are inferred from the push sequence; the mapping (object pointer, string pointer, pid) is likely but not guaranteed.
Whether the Item::bark return value influences anything beyond signalling the event was handled (the code uses it with 'implies' but further effects are unclear).

main_function.name: U8P_KINGBDNS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's 'U8P_KINGBDNS::look' (examine) event: configure object info/processing state and present a short descriptive text ('bed ') to the viewer, then signal that the event was handled and suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the player or actor examines/looks at the object (area-entry or examine trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_KINGBDNS::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_KINGBDNS::look' (examine) interactions on the KINGBDNS object.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine invoked to make an item emit/display a short text or message to a process/player; used here to show the string 'bed '.

external_calls.purpose: Ask the item to emit/display a short text message ('bed ') to the requesting process/player context; return value is captured to indicate success/handling.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_KINGBDNS::look services the object's 'U8P_KINGBDNS::look' (examine) event: it prepares the object for an inspection, makes the item emit a short text 'bed ' (via an Item::bark call), records the bark return value, uses that result to indicate the event was handled, then suspends/returns.
